definitions: accost：走上前去搭话；（常指唐突、冒犯或带威胁地）拦住并接近某人

examples.text: but he knew he had to behave himself. While the pair walked peacefully at first, they were soon accosted by fearsome bandits and Sun Wukong’s old habits bubbled up. Tripitaka scolded the Monkey King, but this only enraged him further.
examples.explanation: 这里的 “were soon accosted by fearsome bandits” 意思是“他们很快被可怕的强盗拦住/上前纠缠”。accost 常强调对方主动靠近并打扰、冒犯或带有威胁感。

source.text: The tale of the Monkey King and the Buddha - Ji Hao
